source: CIS AMAZON WEB SERVICES FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V5.0.0
title: Ensure that IAM External Access Analyzer is enabled for all regions

input_row.source: CIS AMAZON WEB SERVICES FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V5.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure that IAM External Access Analyzer is enabled for all regions
input_row.description: Enable the IAM External Access Analyzer regarding all resources in each active AWS region. IAM Access Analyzer is a technology introduced at AWS reinvent 2019. After the Analyzer is enabled in IAM, scan results are displayed on the console showing the accessible resources. Scans show resources that other accounts and federated users can access, such as KMS keys and IAM roles. The results allow you to determine whether an unintended user is permitted, making it easier for administrators to monitor least privilege access. Access Analyzer analyzes only the policies that are applied to resources in the same AWS Region.
input_row.rationale: AWS IAM External Access Analyzer helps you identify the resources in your organization and accounts, such as Amazon S3 buckets or IAM roles, that are shared with external entities. This allows you to identify unintended access to your resources and data. Access Analyzer identifies resources that are shared with external principals by using logic-based reasoning to analyze the resource-based policies in your AWS environment. IAM External Access Analyzer continuously monitors all policies for S3 buckets, IAM roles, KMS (Key Management Service) keys, AWS Lambda functions, Amazon SQS (Simple Queue Service) queues and more
input_row.audit: From Console: 1. Open the IAM console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/iam/ 2. Under Access analyzer choose External Access 3. Ensure that at least one analyzer is present 4. Ensure that the STATUS is set to Active 5. Repeat these steps for each active region From Command Line: 1. Run the following command: aws accessanalyzer list-analyzers type --<Account|Organization> | grep status  2. Ensure that at least one Analyzer's status is set to ACTIVE. 3. Repeat the steps above for each active region. If an Access Analyzer is not listed for each region or the status is not set to active refer to the remediation procedure below.
input_row.remediation: From Console: Perform the following to enable IAM Access Analyzer for IAM policies: 1. Open the IAM console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/iam/. 2. Choose Access analyzer. 3. Choose Create external access analyzer. 4. On the Create analyzer page, confirm that the Region displayed is the Region where you want to enable Access Analyzer. 5. Optionally enter a name for the analyzer. 6. Optionally add any tags that you want to apply to the analyzer. 7. Choose Create Analyzer. 8. Repeat these step for each active region. From Command Line: Run the following command: aws accessanalyzer create-analyzer --analyzer-name <NAME> --type <ACCOUNT|ORGANIZATION> Repeat this command for each active region. Note: The IAM Access Analyzer is successfully configured only when the account you use has the necessary permissions. References: 1. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/what-is-access- analyzer.html 2. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/access-analyzer-getting- started.html 3. https://awscli.amazonaws.com/v2/documentation/api/latest/reference/accessanal yzer/get-analyzer.html 4. https://awscli.amazonaws.com/v2/documentation/api/latest/reference/accessanal yzer/create-analyzer.html Additional Information: Some regions in AWS are enabled by default, while others are disabled by default. Regions introduced prior to March 20, 2019, are enabled by default and cannot be disabled. Regions introduced afterward can be disabled by default. For more information on managing AWS Regions, please see AWS's documentation on managing AWS Regions.
input_row.references: 1. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/what-is-access- analyzer.html 2. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/access-analyzer-getting- started.html 3. https://awscli.amazonaws.com/v2/documentation/api/latest/reference/accessanal yzer/get-analyzer.html 4. https://awscli.amazonaws.com/v2/documentation/api/latest/reference/accessanal yzer/create-analyzer.html Additional Information: Some regions in AWS are enabled by default, while others are disabled by default. Regions introduced prior to March 20, 2019, are enabled by default and cannot be disabled. Regions introduced afterward can be disabled by default. For more information on managing AWS Regions, please see AWS's documentation on managing AWS Regions.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control specifies auditing whether IAM External Access Analyzer is enabled for all regions, which can be verified using AWS APIs and CLI commands. The audit field references AWS CLI commands to list and verify the status of analyzers in each region, indicating that this can be programmatically checked. Therefore, the control is suitable for an automated approach as it involves checking the configuration of a standard AWS service property (access control mechanisms like IAM Access Analyzer), and AWS APIs provide methods to retrieve this information.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_iam_access_analyzer_enabled_in_all_regions

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the AWS CLI command `aws accessanalyzer list-analyzers` with the `--type <ACCOUNT|ORGANIZATION>` option to list all analyzers for each region.
- Iterate over all active AWS regions to ensure that at least one analyzer is present and has a status of `ACTIVE` in each region.
- Utilize boto3 SDK with `boto3.client('accessanalyzer').list_analyzers()` to programmatically verify the presence and status of analyzers across regions.
- Confirm that the response contains at least one analyzer per region and that its status is `ACTIVE`.
- Pass criteria: At least one analyzer is present and active in each active AWS region.
- Fail criteria: No analyzer found or inactive in any active AWS region.
- This ensures continuous monitoring of resource policies to identify unintended access to resources shared with external principals.

**SOURCE:** CIS AMAZON WEB SERVICES FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V5.0.0
